assertion_results.text: The verification report marks all 5 acceptance criteria as PASS — the diff implements single license filter, comma-separated filter, 400 validation, pagination integration, and unchanged response shape
assertion_results.evidence: In report.md line 12: 'Acceptance Criteria | PASS | 5 of 5 criteria met: single license filter, multi-license filter with OR semantics, 400 for invalid SPDX identifiers, pagination integration with correct filtered totals, and unchanged PaginatedResults<PackageSummary> response shape'. All five criterion files (criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md) each have 'Verdict: PASS' covering single license filter, comma-separated filter, 400 validation, pagination integration, and unchanged response shape respectively.

assertion_results.text: Scope containment is PASS — all files in the diff (list.rs, service/mod.rs, tests/api/package.rs) are listed in the task's Files to Modify or Files to Create
assertion_results.evidence: In report.md line 7: 'Scope Containment | PASS | PR files exactly match the task specification: 2 modified files (`list.rs`, `service/mod.rs`) and 1 new file (`tests/api/package.rs`) with no out-of-scope or unimplemented files'. Detailed findings in Intent Alignment section (lines 27-33) confirm: list.rs (Files to Modify), service/mod.rs (Files to Modify), tests/api/package.rs (Files to Create), with 'No out-of-scope files. No unimplemented files.'

assertion_results.text: No sub-tasks are created because there are no review comments (Review Feedback is N/A)
assertion_results.evidence: In report.md line 5: 'Review Feedback | N/A | No review comments exist on this PR'. Line 6: 'Root-Cause Investigation | N/A | No sub-tasks were created; nothing to investigate'. No mention of sub-task creation anywhere in any output file.

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The outputs directory contains only read-only report files: report.md, criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md. All content is analytical/verification in nature — the report reads and quotes code from the diff but does not modify any source files. The report's overall conclusion (line 19) states 'All checks pass' and presents findings, consistent with a read-only verification skill.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR — it presents findings for human review (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: In report.md line 17: 'Overall: PASS' followed by a summary of findings (lines 19). The report presents findings and verdicts but contains no merge action, no 'gh pr merge' command, no auto-merge instruction. The report concludes with 'Detailed Domain Findings' presenting evidence for human review.

assertion_results.text: Sensitive pattern scan is PASS — the diff contains no passwords, API keys, or private keys
assertion_results.evidence: In report.md line 10: 'Sensitive Patterns | PASS | No secrets, credentials, or sensitive data detected in any added lines; all string literals are standard SPDX license identifiers and test fixture names'. Detailed findings (lines 42-43): 'All added lines were scanned across 6 pattern categories (hardcoded passwords/secrets, API keys/tokens, private keys/certificates, environment files, cloud credentials, database credentials). No matches found.'

assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with ADDITIVE — only new test files were added (tests/api/package.rs is a new file)
assertion_results.evidence: In report.md line 14: 'Test Change Classification | ADDITIVE | Only test file (`tests/api/package.rs`) is entirely new with 4 test functions and 80 lines; no existing tests were modified or removed'. Detailed findings (line 72): 'Test Change Classification (ADDITIVE): The only test file tests/api/package.rs is entirely new (80 lines, 4 test functions). No existing tests were modified, weakened, or removed.'

assertion_results.text: The verification report assembles verdicts from all four domain sub-agents: Scope Containment, Diff Size, and Commit Traceability from Intent Alignment; Sensitive Patterns from Security; CI Status, Acceptance Criteria, and Verification Commands from Correctness; Test Quality (combining Repetitive Test Detection, Test Documentation, and Eval Quality) and Test Change Classification from Style/Conventions
assertion_results.evidence: Report.md summary table contains all expected verdicts: Scope Containment (PASS, line 7), Diff Size (PASS, line 8), Commit Traceability (PASS, line 9), Sensitive Patterns (PASS, line 10), CI Status (PASS, line 11), Acceptance Criteria (PASS, line 12), Test Quality (PASS, line 13), Test Change Classification (ADDITIVE, line 14), Verification Commands (N/A, line 15). Detailed findings are organized by domain: 'Intent Alignment' (lines 27-38) covers Scope Containment, Diff Size, Commit Traceability; 'Security' (lines 41-43) covers Sensitive Pattern Scan; 'Correctness' (lines 45-60) covers CI Status, Acceptance Criteria, Verification Commands; 'Style/Conventions' (lines 63-72) covers Repetitive Test Detection, Test Documentation, Eval Quality, and Test Change Classification. Test Quality in summary (line 13) combines Repetitive Test Detection, Test Documentation, and Eval Quality.

assertion_results.text: The report includes detailed findings with specific evidence for each domain — file-by-file scope comparison (Intent Alignment), line-level pattern scanning results (Security), per-criterion code-level verification (Correctness), and test quality assessment (Style/Conventions) — not just pass/fail verdicts in the summary table
assertion_results.evidence: Intent Alignment (lines 27-38): file-by-file listing with exact paths (list.rs, service/mod.rs, tests/api/package.rs) mapped to 'Files to Modify' or 'Files to Create', plus addition/deletion counts per file. Security (lines 42-43): lists 6 specific pattern categories scanned and notes all string literals are SPDX identifiers and test fixture names. Correctness (lines 48-58): per-criterion verification with code-level details — e.g., criterion 1 mentions spdx::Expression::parse(), is_in filter, InnerJoin; criterion 4 specifies items.len()==2 and total==5 with specific test setup. Style/Conventions (lines 63-72): qualitative test assessment noting 'Each covers a qualitatively different behavior', doc comment pattern verification, and ADDITIVE classification with line/function counts. Additionally, 5 separate criterion files provide extensive code-level evidence.

assertion_results.text: Eval Quality is N/A because no eval result reviews exist in the PR — no reviews match the eval result detection criteria, so Eval Quality does not affect the Test Quality combination
assertion_results.evidence: In report.md line 13: 'Test Quality | PASS | ... Eval Quality: N/A (no eval result reviews on this PR)'. In detailed findings line 70: 'Eval Quality (N/A): No eval result reviews exist on this PR.' Test Quality is PASS despite Eval Quality being N/A, confirming it does not negatively affect the combination.

assertion_results.text: No eval failure sub-tasks are created because Eval Quality is N/A — eval failure sub-tasks are only created when Eval Quality is WARN (at least one eval assertion failed)
assertion_results.evidence: Eval Quality is N/A (report.md line 70: 'Eval Quality (N/A): No eval result reviews exist on this PR.'). No sub-tasks are mentioned anywhere in any output file. Root-Cause Investigation is N/A (line 6: 'No sub-tasks were created; nothing to investigate'), confirming no eval failure sub-tasks were created.

assertion_results.text: Review Feedback and Root-Cause Investigation verdicts are determined by the orchestrator independently from domain analysis — Review Feedback is N/A because no review comments exist, Root-Cause Investigation is N/A because no sub-tasks were created
assertion_results.evidence: In report.md lines 5-6: 'Review Feedback | N/A | No review comments exist on this PR' and 'Root-Cause Investigation | N/A | No sub-tasks were created; nothing to investigate'. These appear in the summary table (lines 5-6) before the domain-specific sections, and are not listed under any of the four domain headings (Intent Alignment, Security, Correctness, Style/Conventions) in the detailed findings (lines 25-72), confirming they are determined independently from domain analysis.
